summary: Successfully demonstrated unauthorized access to Traefik API endpoints when API.Insecure=true is configured. Unauthenticated HTTP requests to /api/rawdata, /api/overview, and /api/http/routers returned sensitive configuration information.
expected_security_impact: Unauthenticated external attacker can access full Traefik configuration including service URLs, routing rules, middleware configurations, and runtime status through API endpoints.
actual_observed_result: HTTP GET requests to API endpoints without authentication credentials successfully returned JSON responses containing full configuration data (routers, services, middlewares, entrypoints). No authentication challenge or rejection occurred.
verification_method: Sent HTTP GET requests to /api/rawdata, /api/overview, and /api/http/routers without any authentication headers. Verified that responses contain JSON configuration data including router definitions and service URLs. Confirmed absence of authentication middleware by checking for WWW-Authenticate headers or 401 responses.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP 200 OK response from /api/rawdata without authentication
JSON response contains 'routers' field with routing configuration
JSON response contains 'services' field with backend service URLs
JSON response contains 'middlewares' field with middleware configurations
No WWW-Authenticate header in response (no authentication challenge)
No 401 Unauthorized status code (request accepted without auth)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the Traefik repository from GitHub, checks out v2.11.0, and mounts it read-only at /target-repo
